info.title: marketing API
info.description:
Multi-channel campaign execution: email drip sequences, social publishing, web content, audience segmentation, and workflow automation.

## Sections

- Campaign Channel Ads
- Campaign Channel Ads Files
- Campaign Channel Ads Notes
- Campaign Channel Ads Tasks
- Campaign Channel Emails
- Campaign Channel Emails Files
- Campaign Channel Emails Notes

- Campaign Channel Ppc

- Campaign Channel Sms

- Campaign Channel Social

- Campaign Channel Web Content

- Segment Files

- Social Publish Job
- Social Publishing
- Web Pages
- Web Publishing
- Website Statuses
- Website Types
- Websites
- Workflow Enrollments
- Workflow Steps
- Workflows

post.summary: Public form submission → create a Lead
post.description:
Public form submission → create a Lead.

POST /api/marketing/preview/:account/:feature/:id/submit

The public preview page posts the collected field values here. For the
`forms` feature we resolve the form, then create a `leads` record from the
submitted First Name / Last Name / Email (the default lead-capture fields
seeded on every form). Every other lead field is optional (V20260603.1), so
a visitor only ever has to supply those three.

Body shape: `{ values: { firstName, lastName, email, ... } }`. We also
accept a bare values map for resilience.

post.summary: OAuth completion. Called by the SPA popup-callback page (`/auth/social-callback`),
post.description:
OAuth completion. Called by the SPA popup-callback page (`/auth/social-callback`),
NOT by the provider directly: the provider redirects the popup to the frontend
route, which reads `code` + `state` from the URL and POSTs them here. We
exchange the code for tokens, list + persist the connectable profiles, register
the live adapter, and return a JSON result the popup relays to its opener via
postMessage. This mirrors the identity OAuth flow (POST /auth/oauth/complete)
and avoids needing raw Express `res` access, which the module-framework route
layer does not provide to service methods.

rootStepIds.description: Workflow-engine entry points. Step IDs with no previousStepIds — branching graphs may have more than one root.

ownerModelKey.description: Polymorphic back-pointer model key (e.g. campaigns, emailSequences).

ownerRecordId.description: ID of the record that owns this workflow (campaign, sequence, etc.).

workflowSteps.description: Embedded step templates for this workflow. Authoritative source for new enrollments; standalone collection is the fallback.

order.description: Position of this step within the workflow (0-based)

webPage.description: The web page associated with this step (landing page, form, etc.)

email.description: The marketing email associated with this step (for email steps)

form.description: The form associated with this step (for form steps)

delayDuration.description: Numeric value for delay steps (e.g. 2 for "2 days")

workflow.description: Parent workflow. Optional during the schema-bridge phase — legacy steps link to campaignSequence instead.

nextStepIds.description: Forward edges in the workflow graph. Empty array marks a leaf. Length > 1 = branching.

previousStepIds.description: Reverse edges. Used by the engine to compute active leaves; empty array marks a root.

recordModelKey.description: Polymorphic pointer: which model the linked record lives in (e.g. forms, webPages, campaignEmails). Denormalised from the typed link fields for indexed trigger lookup.

recordId.description: Polymorphic pointer: ID of the linked record. Denormalised from the typed link fields so a single index covers form/page/email trigger lookups.

socialPost.description: The social post associated with this step (for social steps).

socialSequence.description: Parent social sequence — set for steps owned by a Social Drip Campaign. Mutually exclusive with emailSequence / workflow.

emailLists.description: Legacy segment id array — prefer audienceSegments. type:array (not json) so the module-framework sanitizer persists the string[] instead of dropping it.

audienceSegments.description: Segment ids enrolled when drip is active. string[]. The SELECTED (checked) subset of stagedSegments. type:array (not json) so the module-framework sanitizer persists the string[]. Used by AutoEnrollmentListener and segment-list UI.

stagedSegments.description: Segment ids staged on this drip in the segment-list UI (the list shown to the user). audienceSegments is the selected/enrolled subset of these. string[]. type:array (not json) so the module-framework sanitizer persists the string[].

subjectModelKey.description: Polymorphic subject pointer — the model the enrolled record lives in (e.g. "contacts").

subjectRecordId.description: Polymorphic subject pointer — the ID of the enrolled record (e.g. the contact id).

activeStepIds.description: Denormalised set of step IDs currently on this enrollments active frontier. Engine maintains this on every advance so durable-consumer triggers can fan out without scanning stepStates.

stepStates.description: Per-step runtime state objects. Each: { stepId, status, scheduledFor, processedAt, lastWakeId, lastProcessedEventSeq, error }.

lastProgressAt.description: Updated on every step advance. Watched by the engine for stuck-enrollment detection.
